text:
You are working on beads issue flt-it8.22: "Bookmark fetch SSRF guardrails" (P0 bug).

## Issue Details
Page fetch currently dereferences user-supplied bookmark URLs without network or scheme restrictions. Add SSRF protections: allowlist schemes, reject localhost/private/link-local targets after DNS resolution, cap response size/content type, and add adversarial tests for internal-address, redirect, and DNS-rebinding style cases.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/ssrf-guard"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

When done, push your branch:
```bash
git push -u origin HEAD
```

## Instructions
1. Read CLAUDE.md and api/CLAUDE.md for project conventions
2. Find the page fetch / bookmark capture code (likely in api/internal/worker/ or api/internal/item/)
3. Implement SSRF protections:
   - Allowlist schemes (http, https only)
   - DNS resolution check: reject private, loopback, link-local IPs (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, ::1, fe80::, etc.)
   - Cap response size and validate content type
   - Handle redirects safely (re-check resolved IPs after redirect)
4. Write comprehensive tests including adversarial cases:
   - Internal address bypass attempts
   - Redirect to internal address
   - DNS rebinding patterns
   - Oversized responses
   - Non-HTTP schemes (file://, gopher://, etc.)
5. Follow Go conventions from CLAUDE.md (error wrapping, slog logging, context first param)

## Quality Gate (must pass before reporting done)
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```

## Completion
When done, commit all changes, push your branch, and report back with:
- Summary of changes made
- Quality gate results (paste output)
- Any concerns or follow-up items discovered
